close_reason: Fixed bitmap detail fallback to use the requested route id instead of defaulting to 420000.
description: Bitmap detail pages are opened with route params like 123456.bitmap from the explore grid, but src/lib/api.ts calls the inscription detail API with that route param as inscription_id. The request fails and the fallback always returns MOCK_BITMAPS[0], so every unknown bitmap resolves to 420000.bitmap. Update getBitmap() to interpret bitmap route ids correctly and generate a matching fallback instead of returning the first mock entry.
title: Fix bitmap detail route always falling back to 420000

description:
Create Next.js API route at /api/explore/blocks that returns paginated block heights for a given filter category.

## Endpoint
GET /api/explore/blocks?filter=<category>&page=0&limit=9

## Response shape
{ heights: number[], hasMore: boolean, hasPrev: boolean, total?: number }

## Filter categories to support
- punks: blocks where height % 4 === 3
- palindrome: blocks whose string repr reads the same forwards/backwards
- sub-100k: blocks 0–99,999
- nakamoto: blocks 0–~36,288 (Satoshi mining era)
- patoshi: curated list (stub/placeholder for now)
- billionaire: curated list (stub for now)
- epic-sat: curated list (stub for now)
- pizza: pizza day block neighbourhood (stub for now)
- pristine-punk: curated via external API (stub for now)
- perfect-punk: curated via external API (stub for now)

Deterministic filters (punks, palindrome, sub-100k, nakamoto) can be implemented with pure computation. Others start as stubs returning hardcoded representative blocks until real data is wired up.

File to create: src/app/api/explore/blocks/route.ts
title: Design backend API route for explore page collection filters

description:
Rewrite all copy on /explore to be bitmap-native and less technical. Remove 'WASM-rendered' references. Make loading states feel like art. Changes:

1. src/components/explore/ExploreClient.tsx
   - Heading: 'Block Explorer' → 'Bitmap Explorer'
   - Subheading: 'Bitcoin blocks visualised as WASM-rendered bitmaps' → 'Every Bitcoin block is a bitmap. This is what they look like.'
   - Notable section label: 'Notable' → 'Legendary'
   - Pagination: '← Earlier blocks' → '← Older bitmaps'
   - Pagination: 'Later blocks →' → 'Newer bitmaps →'

2. src/components/explore/BlockSearch.tsx
   - Placeholder: 'Block height…' → 'Jump to any block…'
   - Error: 'Enter a block height between 0 and 1,000,000' → 'Pick a block between 0 and 1,000,000'

3. src/components/explore/BlockCard.tsx
   - Block title format: '\#{height}' → '{height}.bitmap' (e.g. #840000 → 840000.bitmap)
   - Loading state: 'Rendering…' → 'Painting…'
   - Loading state: 'Loading…' → 'Fetching pixels…'
   - Error state: 'Block unavailable' → 'Bitmap not found'

4. src/app/explore/page.tsx
   - Meta description: 'Browse any Bitcoin block as a WASM-rendered bitmap visualization. Explore block data, transaction counts, and listing status.' → 'Every Bitcoin block has a bitmap. Browse them all — from genesis to now.'
title: Explore page copy refresh: bitmap-themed, remove WASM jargon

description: Implement bitmap/[id]/page.tsx Server Component with 2-col layout. Components: MetadataPanel.tsx, ActionPanel.tsx, PriceHistoryChart.tsx (Recharts), RelatedBitmaps.tsx.
title: Phase 3: Detail page

description:
Update ExploreClient.tsx to support collection-based filter chips that change prev/next pagination to move through only matching blocks.

## Changes to src/components/explore/ExploreClient.tsx

### New constants
FILTER_CATEGORIES: FilterCategory[] = [
  { id: 'punks', label: 'Punks' },
  { id: 'palindrome', label: 'Palindrome' },
  { id: 'sub-100k', label: 'Sub 100k' },
  { id: 'patoshi', label: 'Patoshi' },
  { id: 'billionaire', label: 'Billionaire' },
  { id: 'nakamoto', label: 'Nakamoto' },
  { id: 'pizza', label: 'Pizza Block' },
  { id: 'pristine-punk', label: 'Pristine Punk' },
  { id: 'perfect-punk', label: 'Perfect Punk' },
]

### New state
- activeFilter: string | null  (null = sequential mode)
- filterPage: number  (for paginated filtered results)

### Conditional data fetching
When filter active: fetch /api/explore/blocks?filter=X&page=N
When no filter: use existing buildHeights + sequential anchorHeight logic

### Navigation update
- goPrev/goNext: if filter active → change filterPage ±1; else change anchorHeight ±9

### UI additions
- Add filter chips row below Notable blocks row, using home-chip class
- Active chip: bg-[rgba(247,147,26,0.12)] text-primary border-primary/40 styling
- Clicking active chip deselects (back to sequential)
- Header badges: show filter context when active (e.g. 'Punks · Page 3' instead of height range)

## Dependencies
- Requires: backend API route (bitmap-marketplace-frontend-cbx)
- Requires: FilterCategory type (bitmap-marketplace-frontend-d95)
title: Add collection filter chips to explore page with filter-aware pagination

description: Add u_enableRepulsion and u_enableFlicker uniform flags to shaders. When disabled, skip the repulsion distance calculations and flicker branch entirely (not just zero force). This enables the Reduced quality tier.
title: Shader uniform flags for feature toggling

close_reason: All tiers implemented: Full WebGL → Reduced WebGL → Canvas2D → Static image, with FPS-based auto-detection
description: Implement 4-tier quality system: Full WebGL (all effects) → Reduced WebGL (no repulsion/flicker) → Canvas2D → Static image. Monitor FPS via rolling frame time window and auto-downgrade when performance drops.
title: FPS-based quality tier system

description: Add lowest-tier fallback: render one frame with all squares at final positions, convert to image via canvas.toDataURL(), display as . Used when even Canvas2D is too slow.
title: Static poster fallback

close_reason: After code review, the shader uses hardware-accelerated sin/cos for random positions, not iterative hash functions. A DataTexture lookup would not be cheaper for this use case.
description: Replace per-frame hash() computation in vertex shader with a precomputed noise DataTexture (64x64 RGBA). Upload once on init, sample in shader for random start positions and angles.
title: DataTexture for precomputed randomness
